---
title: "Data Science and Digital Skills Programme"
format: html
---

# Data Science & Digital Skills Programme (DS&DS)

The **Data Science & Digital Skills Programme (DS&DS)** is a tiered, in-person training pathway designed to build the core digital competencies, data literacy, and research software skills required for modern, reproducible, and data-intensive research. It supports doctoral researchers at the University of Nottingham in progressing from foundational digital fluency to advanced, domain-specific expertise.

## DS&DSP Interactive Overview

<iframe width="768" height="496" src="https://miro.com/app/live-embed/uXjVIl-8SFk=/?focusWidget=3458764632598762721&embedMode=view_only_without_ui&embedId=142763511172" frameborder="0" scrolling="no" allow="fullscreen; clipboard-read; clipboard-write" allowfullscreen></iframe>


Structured across **foundation**, **intermediate**, and **advanced** tiers, the programme is aligned with the University's strategic priorities in research integrity, digital enablement, and open scholarship. DS&DS is discipline-agnostic, inclusive of all technical backgrounds, and designed to integrate with existing institutional training provision.


---

## Essential Digital Skills (EDS)

**Essential Digital Skills (EDS)** is the foundation tier of the DS&DS programme. Delivered in person during the first year of the doctoral training pathway, EDS establishes baseline knowledge in digital infrastructure, data governance, research data management, and delivery methods.

- [Link to programme](esential_digital_skills/index.html)  

---
title: "Data Science and Digital Skills Programme"
format: html
---

# Data Science & Digital Skills Programme (DS&DS)

The **Data Science & Digital Skills Programme (DS&DS)** is a tiered, in-person training pathway designed to build the core digital competencies, data literacy, and research software skills required for modern, reproducible, and data-intensive research. It supports doctoral researchers at the University of Nottingham in progressing from foundational digital fluency to advanced, domain-specific expertise.

Structured across **foundation**, **intermediate**, and **advanced** tiers, the programme is aligned with the University's strategic priorities in research integrity, digital enablement, and open scholarship. DS&DS is discipline-agnostic, inclusive of all technical backgrounds, and designed to integrate with existing institutional training provision.

The programme will be delivered initially across Year 1 and Year 2 to the 2025 BBSRC Doctoral Training Partnership (DTP) cohort, enabling the development of modular training content with a view to more compact delivery in 2026. The three-tiered curriculum is designed to accommodate diverse levels of prior experience while enabling progression according to individual needs and research context.

- **Foundation**: _Essential Digital Skills_ — mandatory for all students, establishing a shared baseline in digital research literacy.
- **Intermediate**: _Methods for Data Science_ — optional follow-on modules focusing on applied methods and tooling.
- **Advanced**: _Domain-Specific Specialisations_ — tailored to individual research domains and technical trajectories.

This flexible structure enables tailored progression while maintaining curriculum coherence and alignment with the DTP’s aim to prepare researchers for data-intensive, interdisciplinary careers.

---

## Essential Digital Skills (EDS)

**Essential Digital Skills (EDS)** is the foundation tier of the DS&DS programme. Delivered in person during the first year of the doctoral training pathway, EDS establishes baseline knowledge in digital infrastructure, data governance, research data management, and delivery methods. It is mandatory for all DTP researchers and provides the grounding needed to confidently adopt intermediate and advanced methods later in the programme.

- [Link to programme](esential_digital_skills/index.html)
